stories.headline: China praises nations that denied overflight rights for Taiwan President Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar had revoked permission for Mr Lai Ching-te to fly over their territories.
stories.summary: China praised Seychelles, Mauritius, and Madagascar for denying overflight permission to Taiwan President Lai Ching-te's aircraft, citing the "one-China principle." The three African countries had initially granted permission but later revoked it, forcing President Lai to cancel his trip to Eswatini. China expressed "high appreciation" for the countries' decision, stating it reflects their commitment to the one-China principle. Taiwan accused Beijing of pressuring the countries into denying overflight rights. The incident has sparked concern from US lawmakers. President Lai's trip was intended to strengthen ties with Eswatini, one of the few countries that recognizes Taiwan as a sovereign state. The denial of overflight rights has highlighted the ongoing tensions between China and Taiwan.

stories.headline: Trump extends ceasefire with Iran indefinitely at Pakistan's request to allow for diplomatic efforts Trump says the indefinite extension was granted following a request from Pakistan’s prime minister and top military official, as Islamabad continues to work on
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has indefinitely extended a ceasefire with Iran at the request of Pakistan. The extension was granted after Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and top military official asked for more time to facilitate diplomatic efforts. The ceasefire was set to expire, but Trump agreed to hold off on any attacks to allow Pakistan to continue mediating talks between the US and Iran. Pakistan's prime minister thanked Trump for the extension, stating that the country will continue its efforts towards a negotiated settlement. The US had been waiting for a unified proposal from Iran, and the extension is intended to provide more time for diplomatic efforts to bear fruit. The move is seen as a significant development in the ongoing tensions between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: 'Kharg Island storage will be full soon': US masterplan to make Iran bend with Strait of Hormuz blockade The US has been trying to have Iran come to a permanent ceasefire that would put a full stop to the fighting that erupted on February 28.
stories.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced that the US Navy will continue to block Iranian ports, aiming to target Iran's main revenue sources. He warned that Kharg Island's storage facilities will be full soon, leading to the shutdown of Iranian oil wells. Kharg Island handles most of Iran's oil exports and is crucial to US efforts to pressure Tehran. The blockade of Iranian ports and restriction of maritime trade will directly impact the regime's main sources of income. The US is seeking a permanent ceasefire to end the fighting that erupted on February 28. The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz shipping routes is part of the US strategy to pressure Iran into a ceasefire agreement. Bessent's statement was made in a post on social media platform X, emphasizing the US commitment to maintaining the blockade.

stories.headline: Iran’s military says its forces are ‘ready and on the trigger’ if US attacks Iran’s military said early Wednesday that it would immediately strike pre-designated targets if the US launches any new attack on the country, according to stat
stories.summary: Iran's military has stated it is "ready and on the trigger" to immediately strike pre-designated targets if the US launches a new attack on the country. According to state television, Iranian forces are prepared to retaliate against any US attack. This warning comes after Iran's top diplomat stated that the country had learned "valuable lessons" from Israel's military assault in June last year and the subsequent US attacks. Iran has signaled its readiness to respond forcefully to any US attack, while also indicating openness to a new nuclear deal. The situation is being closely monitored, with potential implications for regional war risks, oil market shocks, and nuclear threats. Iranian military forces are on high alert, with their "fingers on the trigger," ready to respond to any perceived threat from the US.

stories.headline: WhatsApp Plus paid subscription trial adds themes, pinned chats and premium stickers
stories.summary: WhatsApp is introducing a premium subscription service called WhatsApp Plus, offering users greater control over the app's appearance and functionality. The subscription, currently being tested, includes features such as custom themes, app icons, and premium sticker packs. Users will also be able to pin up to 20 chats for easier organization and set custom ringtones for individual contacts or conversations. The WhatsApp Plus subscription is available in Android beta for $2.99/month. The service is designed to provide users with expanded personalization options for the interface. The rollout is currently limited to select users, with a broader release expected in the future. The subscription is optional, allowing users to choose whether to pay for the additional features and customization options.

stories.headline: Samsung, LG raise laptop prices twice in three months
stories.summary: Samsung Electronics and LG Electronics have raised laptop prices twice in three months due to a severe shortage of memory chips. The price hikes have resulted in some models increasing by roughly 50 percent compared to the same period last year. The shortage of memory chips is driven by high demand for artificial intelligence (AI) technology, which has led to increased costs for dynamic random-access memory (DRAM). As a result, prices for PCs and smartphones have also increased significantly. The repeated price hikes by Samsung and LG reflect the ongoing impact of the memory chip shortage on the global consumer electronics market, with no signs of relief in sight. The shortage continues to inflate costs across various consumer devices.

stories.headline: OPPO Announces Premium Watch X3 and Enco Clip2 open-ear buds
stories.summary: OPPO has announced the Watch X3, a premium smartwatch running Wear OS, and the Enco Clip2, open-ear earbuds tuned by Dynaudio. The Watch X3 features an AMOLED display, Snapdragon W5 chip, and up to seven days of battery life. The Enco Clip2 earbuds offer dual drivers and up to 40 hours of battery life. Sales for these devices are set to begin on April 24. Additionally, OPPO will also unveil the Enco Air5 Pro, a professional-grade noise-canceling earbud model. These products aim to cater to diverse user lifestyles, from everyday use to professional applications. The Watch X3 and Enco Clip2 are part of OPPO's expanding ecosystem of devices, which will be showcased at the company's upcoming global launch event, along with other products like the Find X9 Ultra and Pad 5.

stories.headline: ‘Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream’ is a delightfully odd escapist fantasy that lives up to the hype
stories.summary: Nintendo's Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream was released globally on April 16, 2026, for the Nintendo Switch. The game is a sequel to the original Tomodachi Life, which was released over a decade ago. Announced in a March 2025 Nintendo Direct, the game brings back the series' unique blend of Miis, slice-of-life antics, and dream-peeking. A recent Nintendo Direct showcased new features for the game, building anticipation for its release. The game allows players to create Miis and engage in various activities, offering a delightfully odd escapist fantasy experience. With its release, fans can now explore the latest installment in the Tomodachi Life series, which has been highly anticipated after a long wait. The game's release marks a grand return for the series, with new features and updates expected to enhance the player experience.

stories.headline: 'Thy go to a part-timer?' Ashwin slams Axar Patel’s captaincy after DC's defeat to SRH Cricket News: Delhi Capitals conceded their highest total of IPL 2026, 242 runs, powered by a sensational unbeaten 135 from Abhishek Sharma, who batted through
stories.summary: Delhi Capitals conceded 242 runs to Sunrisers Hyderabad, their highest total in IPL 2026, with Abhishek Sharma scoring an unbeaten 135. The loss was by 47 runs, which Delhi Capitals captain Axar Patel termed as an "off day". Abhishek Sharma's innings helped SRH set a high target, with DC's bowlers struggling to contain the opposition. Ravichandran Ashwin criticized Axar Patel's captaincy, suggesting it contributed to the defeat. DC's fielding lapses also cost them, according to Axar Patel. The match saw Abhishek Sharma bat through the innings, while DC's bowlers, including Kuldeep Yadav, conceded significant runs, with Kuldeep giving away 22 runs in one over. The defeat has raised questions about DC's strategy and leadership.

stories.headline: Panthers Maul No. 12 Mountaineers in Morgantown, 23 The Pitt Panthers (25-14) run-ruled No. 12 West Virginia (27-10), 23-1, in seven innings in the Backyard Brawl on Tuesday night.
stories.summary: The Pitt Panthers defeated No. 12 West Virginia Mountaineers 23-1 in seven innings in the Backyard Brawl on Tuesday night. The game, played in Morgantown, was a rout with Pitt dominating from the start. The Panthers' 23 runs were scored in just seven innings, invoking the run rule. West Virginia, with a 27-10 record and 12-6 in the Big 12, was heavily favored but failed to show up, allowing Pitt to embarrass them in front of their home crowd. The game got out of hand in the second inning, with the Panthers never looking back. This win improves Pitt's record to 25-14, while West Virginia suffers a significant loss in the rivalry matchup. The Backyard Brawl, a longstanding rivalry between the two universities, saw Pitt come out on top in convincing fashion.

stories.headline: Errors Loom Large in No. 13 Baseball’s 11 TUSCALOOSA, Ala. – No. 13 Alabama baseball (28-14) was unable to overcome an eventual nine-run deficit, as five Crimson Tide errors played a role in Tuesday'
stories.summary: No. 13 Alabama baseball suffered an 11-2 loss to UAB, unable to overcome a nine-run deficit. Five Crimson Tide errors contributed to the defeat, with three errors in the first inning allowing the Blazers to take a 7-0 lead. The loss dropped Alabama's record to 28-14. The team's struggles with errors have been a recurring issue, as seen in their recent 19-3 loss to No. 6 Georgia in seven innings. Alabama's overall record stands at 37-13, with a 13-12 record in the SEC. The team's performance has been closely followed, with live scores, box scores, and updated odds available on ESPN, and comprehensive coverage of the SEC on D1Baseball.

stories.headline: Red Sox To Select Eduardo Rivera The Red Sox are making an aggressive call to add to their bullpen. Read more about Eduardo Rivera at MLB Trade Rumors.
stories.summary: The Boston Red Sox are selecting Eduardo Rivera, a left-handed pitching prospect, to join their bullpen. Rivera was recently promoted from Double-A Portland to Triple-A Worcester, the minors' highest level, after making two starts for Portland to open his 2026 season. The 2026 promotion is sooner than anticipated. Rivera, a native of Puerto Rico, signed with the Red Sox in June 2024, and was re-signed in October 2025, avoiding the Rule 5 Draft. The Red Sox are making an aggressive move to add Rivera to their bullpen, indicating their confidence in his abilities. This decision comes as the team looks to bolster their pitching staff. Rivera's promotion is a significant development in his career, and he is now set to join the Red Sox at the major league level. The move is expected to impact the team's bullpen dynamics.

stories.headline: [India Today] How air defence unit in Sirsa foiled Pak ballistic missile threat during Op Sindoor
stories.summary: A Pakistani ballistic missile, believed to be from the Fateh or Shaheen series, was intercepted over Haryana last May by an Indian Air Force unit in Sirsa. The missile, potentially aimed at Delhi, was neutralized by the Barak-8 surface-to-air missile system operated by 45 Wing. The interception occurred during the Indo-Pak conflict, specifically during Op Sindoor. Led by Air Commodore Rohit Kapil, the unit's quick response prevented a potential devastating strike on Delhi. The successful interception highlighted India's advanced air defence capabilities, demonstrating the effectiveness of its missile systems in countering threats. The incident occurred nearly a year after the Pahalgam terror attack, and details of the operation have only recently been revealed.

stories.headline: [The Times of India] ‘They will never be forgotten’: PM Modi pays tribute to Pahalgam victims on first anniversary
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid tribute to the victims of the 2025 Pahalgam terror attack on its first anniversary. In a post on X, Modi remembered the 26 innocent lives lost, stating they will never be forgotten. He emphasized that India has adopted a doctrine of "decisive and proactive justice," moving beyond mere condemnation of terror. The Prime Minister reiterated India's resolve against terrorism, vowing that the country will never bow to terror. The army also recalled the incident, honoring the victims. The tribute comes a year after the attack, which drew a strong response from India, with the government drawing a red line against terror. Modi's message aimed to reassure that the victims will not be forgotten and that India remains committed to combating terrorism.

stories.headline: [Moneycontrol.com] Tamil Nadu polls 2026: What the DMK, AIADMK and TVK manifestos signal for jobs and startups
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu's 2026 election is a three-way contest between DMK, AIADMK, and TVK. The parties have released manifestos focusing on jobs, artificial intelligence, and youth welfare. Voting is scheduled for April 23, with counting on May 4. The AIADMK alliance is projected to win 114-127 seats, while TVK could play a spoilsport role. A survey shows DMK's Stalin as the most preferred chief ministerial candidate with 41% support, followed by TVK's Vijay with 27.1% and AIADMK's Palaniswami with 24.1%. The election is significant, with the 48-hour silence period underway before polling. The parties' manifestos signal their priorities for jobs and startups, aiming to appeal to young voters. The outcome of the election is uncertain, with TVK's entry adding a new dynamic to the contest. The state's voters will decide the fate of the parties on April 23.

stories.headline: [India Today] TCS Nashik survivor recalls horror, says colleague groped her, abused her gods
stories.summary: A Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) employee in Nashik has alleged sexual harassment and religious discrimination by colleagues. The survivor, an associate at the TCS Nashik branch since July 2024, claimed the harassment began in September 2024 during a training session. She stated that a colleague groped her and abused her gods, creating a hostile workplace environment. The complaint is one of over a dozen cases filed, leading to multiple First Information Reports (FIRs) being registered and arrests made. The employee works in a team handling tele-calling operations for bank credit card holders with outstanding dues. Her account describes the workplace as scary, highlighting the need for a safe and respectful work environment. The allegations have led to a series of investigations and actions against the accused colleagues.

stories.headline: In a turbulent world, India needs to plan for the worst India has two options for scenario writing: NITI Aayog for geopolitics, geoeconomics, etc., and the National Security Council for military scenarios
stories.summary: India is preparing for potential geopolitical and geo-economic challenges amidst a turbulent world. The country has two options for scenario writing: NITI Aayog for geopolitics and geoeconomics, and the National Security Council for military scenarios. A recent national lab in New Delhi, hosted by the FES Office for Regional Cooperation in Asia, brought together thought leaders and strategic experts to discuss India's situation. India's economy has grown significantly over the last 11 years, becoming a key advantage. However, with global alliances fraying and trade, security, and diplomacy pulling in different directions, India must test its strategic reflexes. The country may need to think beyond its current strategies to navigate the complex landscape. Experts and leaders are convening to analyze and plan for potential scenarios, including the worst-case, to ensure India's continued economic rise and national security.

stories.headline: US stocks finished lower as geopolitics remained in focus and potential Islamabad talks collapsed - Newsquawk Asia-Pac Market Open
stories.summary: US stocks finished lower due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly between the US and Iran. The collapse of potential talks in Islamabad contributed to the decline. The USD strengthened as the focus remained on the US-Iran situation, with the Strait of Hormuz being a key point of concern. The waterway, used by oil tankers to exit the Persian Gulf, was re-closed by Iran, pushing oil prices higher. Despite this, the US stock market remains near its recent record, indicating optimism that the US and Iran will avoid a worst-case economic scenario. The duration of the Middle East conflict is seen as a key factor in determining the economic impact. The postponement of US VP Vance's trip to Islamabad and the expiry of the ceasefire have added to market uncertainty.

stories.headline: Tensions Rise: Hezbollah and Israel Exchange Fire Amid Ceasefire Violations The Lebanese armed group Hezbollah and the Israeli military have exchanged fire, each accusing the other of violating a ceasefire.
stories.summary: Hezbollah and the Israeli military have exchanged fire, with each side accusing the other of violating a ceasefire. Hezbollah fired rockets and drones into northern Israel, claiming the Israeli military had breached the ceasefire ahead of US-mediated talks between the Israeli and Lebanese governments. The talks, mediated by the US, aim to address ongoing tensions and discuss Hezbollah's disarmament. Lebanese officials have warned displaced civilians not to return home due to ongoing uncertainty and security risks. The exchange of fire occurred despite a ceasefire being in place, with both sides blaming each other for the violations. The US-mediated talks are set to continue, with the goal of reducing tensions and finding a resolution to the conflict. The situation remains uncertain, with both sides maintaining a high level of alertness.

stories.headline: This Market Rally Has An Elephant In The Room: The AI CapEx Cycle The market narrative is once again shifting from geopolitics to AI. And AI CapEx is driving US equity market returns, like in late 2025.
stories.summary: The US equity market rally is being driven by the AI capital expenditure (CapEx) cycle, similar to late 2025. The market narrative has shifted from geopolitics to AI, with the structural shift to AI CapEx affecting corporate balance sheets. This has led to compressed cash generation and raised funding risks. The stocks of leading hyperscalers have sold off sharply in response to spending plans. According to Lisa Shalett, CIO of Morgan Stanley Wealth Management, the long bull market since 2022 has been a "one-note narrative" driven by the explosion of ChatGPT. The AI "bubble" appears to be approaching its endgame, with the market reacting with caution to the dramatic rise in AI capital expenditure by technology hyperscalers. This shift has resulted in stock concentration in US equities, posing risks to investors. The market's reaction is being closely watched as the AI CapEx cycle continues to unfold.

stories.headline: India’s Sensex, Nifty fall 1% on Middle East tensions The indices were affected by the ongoing tensions in the Middle East
stories.summary: India's Sensex and Nifty indices fell by 1% due to ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The decline was also attributed to rising crude oil prices, FII outflows, and weak global cues, which dampened investor sentiment. Top losers on the Sensex included L&T, Bharat Electronics, HCL Technologies, Kotak Mahindra Bank, and Hindustan Unilever, each declining around 1%. In contrast, Trent, the parent company of Zudio, gained over 3%. The BSE Sensex slipped over 650 points, with IT stocks contributing to the weakness. The Indian markets have been volatile, tracking global market trends and reacting to developments in the Middle East, including US-Iran talks. The Nifty 50 is expected to potentially surpass its 50-day exponential moving average this month, according to some market analysts.

stories.headline: Stock markets today: Sensex, Nifty50 fall in early trade dragged by IT firms Indian benchmark equity indices, Sensex and Nifty, experienced a decline in early trade, ending a three-day rally, primarily due to heavy selling in IT stocks a
stories.summary: Indian benchmark equity indices, Sensex and Nifty, declined in early trade on Wednesday, ending a three-day rally. The decline was primarily due to heavy selling in IT stocks. The BSE Sensex slipped over 650 points, while the Nifty 50 fell below its previous close. Foreign fund outflows also contributed to the decline. The sell-off in IT stocks dragged the market sentiment lower, with investors booking profits after a sharp recent rally. Geopolitical tensions remained a concern, with developments such as Donald Trump extending the ceasefire deadline. The decline in Indian markets was in line with the weak sentiment in IT stocks, which faced heavy selling pressure. The market decline was a correction after a three-day rally, with investors cautious due to global and domestic factors.

stories.headline: Stock Market Updates: Sensex Down 300 Points, Nifty Below 24,500; IT Stocks Weigh The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex declined as investors grew cautious after the United States and Iran failed to reach an agreement
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 declined as investors grew cautious after the US and Iran failed to reach an agreement. The Sensex slipped 300 points, while the Nifty fell below 24,500. This decline comes after a 7-day rally in the market. The Sensex had previously slipped 705.97 points to 80,080.57, and the Nifty50 fell 211.15 points to 24,500.9, amid escalating trade tensions between India and the US. The tensions were triggered by India's continued imports of Russian crude oil, leading to a 50% duty on certain imports. In pre-opening trade, the Sensex had declined by 270 points to 81,323, and the Nifty dropped 79 points to 24,733, indicating a cautious market sentiment. The decline in IT stocks also weighed on the Nifty, contributing to the overall market downturn.

stories.headline: Stock Market LIVE: GIFT Nifty signals negative open; Asia mkts fall on failed US-Iran peace talks Sensex Today | Stock Market LIVE Updates, April 22: The GIFT Nifty indicated a negative open for the Nifty50 as the US and Iran talks failed to produce a resolu
stories.summary: The GIFT Nifty indicated a negative open for the Nifty50 as the US and Iran talks failed to produce a resolution. This development has led to a decline in Asian markets, with investors fearing a prolonged geopolitical situation. The Nifty futures traded 40 points, or 0.16%, lower at 25,990, hinting at a negative start for the domestic market. The failed US-Iran peace talks have sparked concerns among investors, leading to a fall in global markets. The GIFT Nifty, an innovative financial product launched by the National Stock Exchange, provides an opportunity for global investors to participate in India's market. As of now, the GIFT Nifty signals a negative opening, with investors closely watching the situation and its potential impact on the market. The current price and technical levels of the GIFT Nifty are being closely monitored by investors and market analysts.

stories.headline: Oil shock complicates central bank outlooks
stories.summary: The Middle East conflict has driven oil prices above $100 per barrel, complicating global central banks' outlooks. Elevated oil prices are expected to persist, posing significant risks to inflation and challenging central banks to respond to accelerating inflation and slowing growth. The US Federal Reserve may need to adjust its policy as high oil prices are likely to keep underlying inflation above the 2% target for the rest of the year. Fixed-income investors should prepare for potential changes in interest rate expectations and increased volatility in global bond markets. The oil shock has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz, stoking inflation fears and testing central banks' ability to shore up growth while managing inflation risks. Central banks face a difficult decision on how to balance growth and inflation concerns amidst the ongoing oil price surge.

stories.headline: HCL Tech share price tank over 9% after weak Q4
stories.summary: HCL Technologies' share price fell 9.85% to ₹1,299 on the NSE after the company's Q4 results missed expectations. The IT major reported a profit of ₹4,488 crore and 12.3% revenue growth, but sequential weakness and constant currency decline weighed on sentiment. The company guided for 1-4% growth in FY27, citing weak discretionary demand and client-specific headwinds. Brokerages responded by cutting targets and downgrading earnings, striking a cautious tone on the company's mixed Q4 performance and softer-than-expected FY27 outlook. The weak guidance shocked markets, prompting a sharp decline in the stock's price. Despite year-over-year growth, the company's sequential performance and FY27 guidance disappointed D-Street expectations, leading to a significant drop in the share price. Multiple brokerages have trimmed their targets, reflecting growth concerns and a muted outlook for the company.

stories.headline: 4 MBB Consultants Quit to Be AI Founders and Had to Unlearn Consulting
stories.summary: Four former consultants from top firms McKinsey, Bain, and BCG quit to found AI startups. They leveraged their consulting experience, but also had to unlearn certain skills to succeed in the fast-moving AI industry. The transition from consulting to entrepreneurship was a natural next step for some, who wanted to own a business and build something rather than just recommend strategies. The move highlights the talent pipeline from consulting to AI entrepreneurship, where traditional business skills can be both beneficial and hindering. Many MBB consultants redeploy their skills in new roles, with some choosing smaller firms for greater ownership and speed, while entrepreneurship remains a minority path, but one that is gaining traction, particularly in the AI sector. This trend challenges the traditional dominance of the consulting world by MBB and the Big Four.

stories.headline: Air Canada jet forced into emergency climb after near‑miss with U.S. flight at JFK airport
stories.summary: An Air Canada jet was forced into an emergency climb after a near-miss with a US flight at JFK airport on Monday afternoon. The incident occurred around 2:30 pm when Republic Airways Flight 4464, operating as American Airlines, and Jazz Aviation Flight 554, operating for Air Canada, came within 350 feet of each other while approaching parallel runways. The American Airlines regional jet was on final approach to runway 31L from Indianapolis, while the Air Canada Express flight was approaching runway 31R from Toronto. Controllers warned of a potential collision, prompting both flights to abort their landings. The close call is the second such incident in recent days at a major US airport, highlighting concerns over air traffic control and safety protocols. An investigation into the incident is likely to follow. The flights were safely redirected after the near-miss.

stories.headline: Florida launches criminal probe into OpenAI and ChatGPT over deadly shooting
stories.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier has launched a criminal investigation into OpenAI and its artificial intelligence app ChatGPT. The probe is related to a deadly shooting at Florida State University last year. The decision to launch the investigation comes after prosecutors reviewed conversation logs between ChatGPT and the accused gunman. The attorney general's office is investigating the alleged role of ChatGPT in the mass shooting. The Office of Statewide Prosecution will handle the case, which may lead to potential criminal charges against OpenAI. The investigation marks an escalation of Florida's dispute with OpenAI over the popular chatbot's potential involvement in the deadly incident. The state is examining the chat logs to determine if ChatGPT played a role in the shooting.

stories.headline: Florida’s attorney general launches criminal probe into ChatGPT over FSU shooting
stories.summary: Florida's Attorney General, James Uthmeier, has launched a criminal investigation into OpenAI's ChatGPT. The probe is related to the artificial intelligence app's interactions with a gunman who killed two people and wounded six others at Florida State University last year. The investigation was initiated after an initial review of chat logs between ChatGPT and the gunman. Prosecutors are examining whether ChatGPT provided advice or guidance to the shooter. The Office of Statewide Prosecution is handling the case, which marks a rare criminal investigation into an AI app. The investigation aims to determine if there was any criminal behavior involving ChatGPT's interactions with the gunman. The incident has raised concerns about the potential role of AI in violent crimes and the need for accountability. The probe is ongoing, with no further details released at this time.

stories.headline: OpenAI Consults With Accenture, PwC, Capgemini To Sell Codex To Businesses
stories.summary: OpenAI is expanding partnerships with major consulting firms to accelerate enterprise adoption of its Codex artificial intelligence tools. The company is collaborating with firms such as Accenture, Capgemini, and PwC to scale its coding model, Codex. A new program, Codex Transformation Partners, has been announced, bringing in several professional services firms to help large organizations deploy Codex across their software development pipelines. Global systems integrators, including CGI, Cognizant, Infosys, and Tata Consultancy Services, are also part of the partnership. This move aims to speed up the adoption of Codex, which has already reached four million weekly active users, up from three million just two weeks ago. The partnerships will enable large companies to effectively deploy and utilize Codex, further expanding its reach in the enterprise sector.

stories.headline: No fourth headline available
stories.summary: No fourth federal stimulus check has been authorized by Congress or announced by the IRS as of May 2025. Despite this, rumors and misinformation about a potential "fourth stimulus check" continue to circulate online. Misleading headlines, viral posts, and scam messages have spread false information, causing confusion among the public. The lack of official confirmation from the government has not stopped the speculation, with many online sources perpetuating the rumors. The IRS and Congress have not made any announcements about a new stimulus payment, and individuals should be cautious of scams and false information. Official updates on stimulus payments will come from reputable sources, including the IRS and government websites.
